task_completion.description: Agent opens and reads the sampling_points.csv file to get the GPS coordinates.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, spreadsheet viewer, or terminal window displaying the contents of /home/ga/Documents/QGC/sampling_points.csv with the 6 coordinate pairs.

task_completion.description: Agent starts the mission plan with a Takeoff command.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the mission planner interface (e.g., QGroundControl), the first item in the mission sequence list is 'Takeoff'.

task_completion.description: Agent adds 6 waypoints/loiter points corresponding to the coordinates from the CSV.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The mission sequence list shows 6 distinct waypoint or loiter items added after the takeoff command, or the map shows 6 points plotted.

task_completion.description: Agent sets the altitude to 30m and the loiter time to 30 seconds for the waypoints.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The waypoint parameter settings panel in the GUI shows 'Altitude' set to 30 (or within 15-50m tolerance) and 'Hold' or 'Loiter Time' set to 30 seconds.

task_completion.description: Agent ends the mission with a Return to Launch (RTL) command.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The final item in the mission sequence list is 'Return', 'Return to Launch', or 'RTL'.

task_completion.description: Agent saves the mission plan to the specified file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog or file manager showing the mission being saved as 'soil_sampling.plan' in the '/home/ga/Documents/QGC/' directory, or a sync/save confirmation in the GUI.

integrity.description: Agent used a graphical mission planner (like QGroundControl) to create the mission.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the QGroundControl GUI (or similar drone mission planning software) rather than a text editor writing the raw JSON .plan file.

integrity.description: Agent manually entered coordinates or clicked on the map to create waypoints.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent typing coordinates into the waypoint editor or clicking the map interface, rather than running a background script to generate the mission.

integrity.description: Agent created the plan from scratch rather than copying an existing file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The mission is built step-by-step in the GUI, starting from an empty or default mission, rather than just opening a pre-made 'soil_sampling.plan' file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target coordinates from the CSV are: [47.3972, 8.5435], [47.3978, 8.5435], [47.3984, 8.5435], [47.3984, 8.5455], [47.3978, 8.5455], [47.3972, 8.5455]. The mission must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/QGC/soil_sampling.plan. The target altitude is 30m (acceptable range 15m-50m) and the loiter time must be at least 20 seconds (target 30s).
